clean_body: If you have a minute, President Trump & Elon Musk would LOVE to hear from you. | | | | | | Peter, President Trump’s has saved the American people over since its inception in January. | | | | | FL-03 Rep. Kat Cammack has had the honor of working alongside President Trump and Elon Musk to direct D.O.G.E. and maximize taxpayer savings through the department’s work. And if we stay on this current trajectory, DJT’s Department of Government Efficiency will save American citizens over by the end of Trump’s second term in January 2029. Peter, your grassroots support is the fuel to our fire when it comes to reining in the corrupt Fed and delivering a government that works for by the time President Trump leaves office. | | | | | Thank you for supporting the American people’s fight to REIN IN the corrupt Fed. For Liberty, Team Cammack | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- | | | | Meet Kat Kat Cammack is currently serving her third term representing the FREE state of Florida in the People’s House as FL-03 Congresswoman. And her road from the small cattle ranch she grew up on to the halls of Congress in our nation’s capital has been far from ordinary. A third generation commercial sandblaster, Kat originally aspired to take over her family’s business and continue the rural, rodeo lifestyle she had grown so accustomed to. That was until a failed Obama-era housing policy caused Kat’s family to lose their ranch, leaving her and her mother homeless. They lived in their car and bounced from cheap motel to cheap motel just to keep a roof over their heads. But overcoming these hardships moulded Kat into the gritty and unapologetic leader she is today. After her family’s livelihood was destroyed by big government, she made it her life mission to deliver federal reform in the DC Swamp for the American people. So she packed her belongings and moved to Florida’s 3rd Congressional District – where she would serve as a Deputy Chief of Staff for nearly a decade before running for Congress herself. After emerging victorious in a brutal, 10-way GOP primary contest, the people of FL-03 elected Kat to serve them in the People’s House in November of 2020. Throughout her journey from homelessness to the House of Representatives, Kat Cammack has shown unwavering and unapologetic grit in her fight for the American people. And she has no plans of backing down until we truly have a government for the people, by the people once again here in the United States. When she’s not going toe-to-toe with the Washington Swamp, Kat enjoys spending time with her loving husband Matt – who serves as a firefighter and SWAT Medic with the Gainesville Fire Rescue. In the little spare time that they have, you can find Kat and Matt fishing, skeet shooting, or watching Gators football. They are incredibly proud to call north central Florida’s “Gator Nation” home. Go Gators, and let’s keep Florida the most FREE state in the union! | | | | A Message From Congresswoman Kat, to You Peter, the opportunity to fight for YOU in the Washington Swamp has been the greatest honor of my life. If we’re going to guarantee a free and prosperous country for future generations of Americans, we need to shake up the status quo in our nation’s capital by cutting reckless government spending, securing our borders, and putting America FIRST in all facets of government operation once again. Can I count on you to chip in just $5 today so we can usher in a NEW era of leadership in our nation’s capital this year? | | | | | | | | | | Peter, my gratitude for your financial contributions can’t be put into words. But donating is just one of many ways you can get involved in the good fight for America’s future. name: Indiana GOP
subject: A Week of Action
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE INDIANA REPUBLICAN STATE COMMITTEE
body:
| | |
---
| |
---
| | _**A Week of Action**_
---
Friends, This has been an exciting week for the Indiana GOP as we have elected new state executive officers and came together in Indianapolis for our annual Spring Dinner. Governor Braun joined us to welcome Oklahoma Governor Kevin Stitt as our keynote speaker. Together, they delivered a conservative message to the nearly 1,000 Hoosier Republicans in attendance. Thank you to all who attended and supported the event as we gathered to celebrate our party's successes and look forward to a bright future. Governor Braun has made lowering property taxes for homeowners, farmers, veterans, seniors, and small businesses a priority throughout his campaign and administration. This week, the Governor and legislative leaders agreed on a plan that would deliver historic relief to Hoosiers. While the legislative process continues to play out, we support the efforts of our leaders in Indianapolis. As the legislative session moves toward its end, Governor Braun has signed dozens of bills into law making government more efficient, responsive, and responsible for Hoosiers. We applaud the work of the Governor and our General Assembly to make Indiana a beacon of freedom and opportunity for all. Following our recent severe weather, flooding, and tornadoes throughout the state, Governor Braun has declared a state of emergency in several counties to give local leaders, businesses, and citizens access to the resources that they need to recover. The prayers and support of all of us here at the Indiana GOP are with those affected by these events. Our Republican leadership has taken decisive action to support, protect, and empower Hoosiers, and the Indiana GOP is proud to stand with them in these efforts. As always, thank you for being a part of our team. Please feel free to reach out if there's anything I can assist with.
---
| |
---
| | Lana Keesling, Indiana GOP Chairwoman
---
|
---
|
---
| |
---
| |
---
_**Governor Braun Announces Agreement to Bring Historic Property Tax Relief to Hoosiers**_
---
**Governor Mike Braun** after months of work and negotiation to deliver property tax relief for Hoosiers, Governor Braun reached and agreement with the House GOP to move forward. **What he said** : “I am proud to announce that with Amendment 36 to Senate Bill 1, we have agreed upon a plan to bring historic property tax relief to Hoosiers. I am grateful for the leadership of Speaker Todd Huston and President Pro Tem Rod Bray and for the ways they have partnered with us to deliver this meaningful tax relief to Hoosiers. I encourage House members to support this amendment and urge the Senate to then take action quickly to get it to my desk for signature." \- **Governor Braun** , via a [press release](http://links.email.in-gop.com/s/c/0mtVg17e8f32AAhyseOmdr4xWLwxoQtqSUFJaxd4te0IduijHFEckMO7jrZzBtGxZzzv1V8iOTO7c0JgNS3GxdtiBfKyDyzDIW-TOrOIS-7fiqOFNf0lDjbIKFScVHKMKiFIrk-tsdBZa48IqxvqJCkDH8IzfOuMRPVgxC48svuwT_4qXwWMSDaD9O4hJIdqHMN1Iq5Lbamuu1_eGZ1pDv82NMtQF2lKJ635weHNbyzzFGI5OjEF7zClHNf946zs6PHm_3jjTb70ZMwM3Ow7IXdfzX6bLKcFSGa-gpmHaynQxzkn18fP9COauB5wzHZmB8iQh-1tbAHDicV0VEKqrNcn3TgA8-ONOg4aiKIU3EgndCaeIbd66jboold85dYvW3MaV1keH_39MrJ_n65grkhRCR3nUaMVvMJdsU9VD59mk5AiCCKjWXkoQOu50RR09kDahh3NFP8A32ed2T1dIfQCCvN92A1kFZDP/h1enHs9ya3CNMHQjiJoVGv9puYBWMtYv/7).
---
---
**_Governor Braun Issues Executive Orders to Improve Retention of Correctional Officers and Reduce Recidivism_**
---
**Governor Mike Braun** on Wednesday, signed two executive orders centered around the Indiana Department of Correction. The first aims to help inmates secure gainful employment after they are released, to lower the rates of inmates going directly from incarceration to homelessness and reoffending. The second directs the Indiana Department of Correction to create a comprehensive employee retention plan by enhancing training, gathering staff feedback, and using data to identify and address high-turnover areas.
**What he said** : “Hoosiers are safer when inmates have a clear pathway toward gainful employment and stable housing after they are released. This executive order aims to lower the reoffending rate by helping formerly incarcerated individuals find work and avoid homelessness.” and “Our corrections officers have a really tough job, and do it exceptionally well. Today, I’m taking action to make sure we retain our great correctional employees with a comprehensive approach to employee retention: we’ll be improving training, listening to employees’ feedback on their work environment and leadership effectiveness, and using data to identify high turnover areas where we can improve.” \- **Governor****Braun,** via a [press release](http://links.email.in-gop.com/s/c/j3VwXNrY0dT15I7PVb_7as7BeIuJLHi2wNAceCknRLbvdh5jtsHIve9zOXfQF-sjwN2sSoBlzxriX5ip8xYEv7CYd5xNiRjXRJ2vQVCflNOVl6XNZXo0ZyB4VDthQQbIMnnYSz1Y5nz2oKoXFazv1PM2r5jLoV1Vbsdue9Rk8yN_BsvlyVY7zdnLG11eSWqt5ZwFuYjEovAhKn0B4Lbeoma7j-JQ623wXy5GPfuGR-m-kbTFCifgbgS2rYnOBe-nvTr6YqjjpPHB8_c510cwKPyruKq7zK7jDdHCw9jYPuNN7OBrNE7H_l0Gp59OGgnViaXY_oS_y8-qbAzMTeFWP9uqs4hCpdiXJJnz7GFQb6ktm8Fscsoh-eF8xoXGb23g3_RlVaB_BLJysImXKpbx_qz-RYjK37Qj04v-_LLMT8Vc0fmY_-e4ywPMrZ_RA9T_6Nj07wwbhWY9GjXPpFwwhgDoBY16kplsLjEFwPagiluQTQ/m31rHPtkkn9QSQdGsZe4hYyVjZWtVlMA/7).

# Introducing our New Officers
---
---
**Chairwoman Lana Keesling** is from Fort Wayne, serving as City Clerk. She previously held an executive role at a privately held company, overseeing finance, information technology, and human resources. Keesling is an alumna of the Richard G. Lugar Excellence in Public Service Series. **Vice-Chairman Jim Johnson** is the Chair of the Montgomery County GOP, he currently works as the Director of Intergovernmental Affairs for INDOT and has previously worked as a legislative aide in the General Assembly. He is an active member and 4th District Chair of the Indiana Federation of Young Republicans. **Secretary Aaron Minnich** is a native of New Albany and now lives in Marion County. He formerly served as Assistant Secretary for the Indiana Republican Party. Minnich has also served as Vice Chairman and Treasurer of the Floyd County GOP and was Chairman of the Indiana University Southeast College Republicans. **Treasurer Erin Lucas** lives in Hendricks County and served as Interim Chairwoman of the Indiana Republican Party. She has worked in non-profit development, management, and as a Congressional aide.

**SEA 74:** Provides that an individual who is: (1) reasonably believed to be suffering from a health condition which is the direct result of alcohol consumption; and (2) assisted by a person who requested emergency medical assistance for the individual; is immune from prosecution for certain crimes. **SEA 95****:** Allows the state, a state agency, or a political subdivision (public employer) to be reimbursed for the costs of employing and training a law enforcement officer by a public employer that subsequently employs the officer. Provides that the reimbursement amount decreases over time and is eliminated three years after the officer is certified by the law enforcement training board to act as an officer. Allows a public employer to require, as a condition of hiring, that the individual enter into a contract or agreement to reimburse the public employer for employment and training costs, if the individual is subsequently hired by a private or governmental employer that is not defined by statute as a public employer. **SEA 144:** Requires in a county other than Marion County, that the county police department or a designee of the county police department make an annual presentation regarding the county police force pension trust and trust fund to the county council. **SEA 218:** Amends the information that must be provided by a supervising agency in quarterly reports to the local justice reinvestment advisory council. Requires the statewide justice reinvestment advisory council to compile the quarterly reports and electronically transmit an annual report to the legislative council and to the judicial conference of Indiana not later than May 1(instead of March 15) of each year. **SEA 219: **Provides that a person who, not having a contractual interest in the property, knowingly or intentionally enters the real property of another person after having been denied entry by the other person, that person's agent, or a law enforcement officer acting on behalf of the other person or their agent, commits criminal trespass, a Class A misdemeanor. Provides that a person who, not having a contractual interest in the property, knowingly or intentionally refuses to leave the real property of another person after having been asked to leave by the other person, that person's agent, or a law enforcement officer acting on behalf of the other person or the other person's agent, commits criminal trespass, a Class A misdemeanor. **SEA 426:** Expands IURC oversight to include certain water utilities with repeated environmental violations, updates utility acquisition and rate recovery rules, and protects utilities from damage claims if water meets regulatory standards. Also restricts the Department of Natural Resources from ordering dam closures that would affect public water service or private wells. **SEA 457:** Sets guidelines and requirements for carbon dioxide transmission pipeline certification, including contractor qualifications and fee reallocations. Updates regulations on carbon sequestration projects, establishes related fees and penalties, authorizes investigatory well permits, and redirects various revenues to the state general fund. **SEA 486:** Requires sheriffs to help eligible individuals apply for Medicaid before release from certain facilities and ensures continuity of Medicaid services for juveniles post-release. Also includes insurer response timelines, Medicaid claim rules, creation of a Medicaid beneficiary advisory commission, updated child care provider training requirements, and changes to advisory council memberships. **HEA 1016:** Provides for the appointment of a fire chief, or the fire chief's designee, to a county school safety commission. **HEA 1033:** Changes the maximum amount of retainage for certain state and local public works projects from: (1) 10% to 6% of the dollar value of all work satisfactorily completed until the public work is 50% complete; and (2) 5% to 3% of the dollar value of all work satisfactorily completed until the public work is substantially complete. Removes the requirement of a minimum amount of retainage for certain state and local public works projects. **HEA 1065:** Allows an organization or entity to establish and administer a cancer clinical trial participation program for the purpose of providing payments to a participant for certain costs incurred by the participant while participating in a cancer clinical trial. Sets forth program requirements and participant eligibility. **HEA 1099:** Provides that the maximum age for a safe haven infant is 60 days of age. Repeals the definition of "abandoned child". Amends the definition of "foster youth". Makes conforming changes. **HEA 1102:** Removes language restricting school corporations from entering into a contract with a religiously affiliated nonprofit preschool program. **HEA 1137:** Requires a court to expunge certain records related to the red flag law if the court finds that an individual is not dangerous, and permits a court to expunge certain records related to the red flag law if the court finds that an individual previously found dangerous is no longer dangerous. **HEA 1148:** Provides that a registration or certificate of a birth or stillbirth is open to public inspection and copying upon the request of any person that occurs 99 years (instead of 75 years) after the record is created. **HEA 1200:** Provides that if a prescriber issues a prescription for an opioid for administration through an intrathecal pump or epidural pain pump: (1) the prescription may not exceed a 180 day supply; and (2) the patient may not be required to have a face-to-face visit with the prescriber more than once every 180 days unless the prescriber determines an earlier follow up visit is medically necessary. **HEA 1320:** Provides that an individual who completes a United States Department of Labor registered apprenticeship program for barbers or manicurists is eligible to take the licensure examination of the applicable profession. **HEA 1554:** Specifies that the penalty for a operating a motor vehicle while under a lifetime forfeiture of driving privileges is: (1) a Level 6 felony, if the forfeiture occurred before July 1, 2015; and (2) a Level 5 felony, if the forfeiture occurred after June 30, 2015. **HEA 1572:** Adds speech-language pathology assistant to the definition of "practitioner" for purposes of the provision of telehealth. Specifies services included in the definition of "audiology". Allows for the sale, use, provision of customer service, or distribution of an over-the-counter hearing aid without the fitting of the hearing aid by a hearing aid dealer who has been issued a certificate of registration or a licensed audiologist. **HEA 1626:** Requires the following to be included in a custody order: (1) a trial court shall include the court's findings of fact and conclusions of law on which the custody order is based; and (2) an appellate decision shall include the facts upon which the appellate court relied to affirm or reverse an order. **SEA 127:** Prohibits a county, city, or town from adopting or enforcing an ordinance or resolution that prohibits or imposes requirements on certain battery charged security devices. **SEA 221:** Changes certain references from "auditor of state" to: (1) "state comptroller (referred to as "auditor of state" in the Constitution of the State of Indiana)"; or (2) "state comptroller (auditor of state)". Requires "state comptroller (auditor of state)" to be listed on the 2026 general election ballot **SEA 450: **Renames "delegate" to "commissioner" for Indiana's representation at an Article V convention and sets appointment requirements, including residency, lobbying restrictions, and criminal background. It also outlines the selection of commissioners, chair appointment, ethical restrictions, and mandates a joint resolution limiting their authority to amend certain constitutional provisions. **SEA 484:** Sets forth requirements for recreational vehicle dealer agreements for the sale of recreational vehicles in Indiana. **SEA 524:** Allows an Indiana resident of a contiguous county to be appointed the head of the department of law.

clean_body: | | | | | | | A Week of Action Friends, This has been an exciting week for the Indiana GOP as we have elected new state executive officers and came together in Indianapolis for our annual Spring Dinner. Governor Braun joined us to welcome Oklahoma Governor Kevin Stitt as our keynote speaker. Together, they delivered a conservative message to the nearly 1,000 Hoosier Republicans in attendance. Thank you to all who attended and supported the event as we gathered to celebrate our party's successes and look forward to a bright future. Governor Braun has made lowering property taxes for homeowners, farmers, veterans, seniors, and small businesses a priority throughout his campaign and administration. This week, the Governor and legislative leaders agreed on a plan that would deliver historic relief to Hoosiers. While the legislative process continues to play out, we support the efforts of our leaders in Indianapolis. As the legislative session moves toward its end, Governor Braun has signed dozens of bills into law making government more efficient, responsive, and responsible for Hoosiers. We applaud the work of the Governor and our General Assembly to make Indiana a beacon of freedom and opportunity for all. Following our recent severe weather, flooding, and tornadoes throughout the state, Governor Braun has declared a state of emergency in several counties to give local leaders, businesses, and citizens access to the resources that they need to recover. The prayers and support of all of us here at the Indiana GOP are with those affected by these events. Our Republican leadership has taken decisive action to support, protect, and empower Hoosiers, and the Indiana GOP is proud to stand with them in these efforts. As always, thank you for being a part of our team. Please feel free to reach out if there's anything I can assist with. | | | | Lana Keesling, Indiana GOP Chairwoman | | | | | | Governor Braun Announces Agreement to Bring Historic Property Tax Relief to Hoosiers Governor Mike Braun after months of work and negotiation to deliver property tax relief for Hoosiers, Governor Braun reached and agreement with the House GOP to move forward. What he said : “I am proud to announce that with Amendment 36 to Senate Bill 1, we have agreed upon a plan to bring historic property tax relief to Hoosiers. I am grateful for the leadership of Speaker Todd Huston and President Pro Tem Rod Bray and for the ways they have partnered with us to deliver this meaningful tax relief to Hoosiers. I encourage House members to support this amendment and urge the Senate to then take action quickly to get it to my desk for signature." \- Governor Braun , via a . Governor Braun Issues Executive Orders to Improve Retention of Correctional Officers and Reduce Recidivism Governor Mike Braun on Wednesday, signed two executive orders centered around the Indiana Department of Correction. The first aims to help inmates secure gainful employment after they are released, to lower the rates of inmates going directly from incarceration to homelessness and reoffending. The second directs the Indiana Department of Correction to create a comprehensive employee retention plan by enhancing training, gathering staff feedback, and using data to identify and address high-turnover areas. What he said : “Hoosiers are safer when inmates have a clear pathway toward gainful employment and stable housing after they are released. This executive order aims to lower the reoffending rate by helping formerly incarcerated individuals find work and avoid homelessness.” and “Our corrections officers have a really tough job, and do it exceptionally well. Today, I’m taking action to make sure we retain our great correctional employees with a comprehensive approach to employee retention: we’ll be improving training, listening to employees’ feedback on their work environment and leadership effectiveness, and using data to identify high turnover areas where we can improve.” \- GovernorBraun, via a . | Introducing our New Officers Chairwoman Lana Keesling is from Fort Wayne, serving as City Clerk. She previously held an executive role at a privately held company, overseeing finance, information technology, and human resources. Keesling is an alumna of the Richard G. Lugar Excellence in Public Service Series. Vice-Chairman Jim Johnson is the Chair of the Montgomery County GOP, he currently works as the Director of Intergovernmental Affairs for INDOT and has previously worked as a legislative aide in the General Assembly. He is an active member and 4th District Chair of the Indiana Federation of Young Republicans. Secretary Aaron Minnich is a native of New Albany and now lives in Marion County. He formerly served as Assistant Secretary for the Indiana Republican Party. Minnich has also served as Vice Chairman and Treasurer of the Floyd County GOP and was Chairman of the Indiana University Southeast College Republicans. Treasurer Erin Lucas lives in Hendricks County and served as Interim Chairwoman of the Indiana Republican Party. She has worked in non-profit development, management, and as a Congressional aide. | Indiana GOP Spring Dinner Madison County Chairwoman's Breakfast Indiana Federation of Republican Women Spring Board Meeting Adams County Lincoln Day Gala Our Committee Leaders Chairwoman's County Visits: 21 | Indiana GOP is Hiring Interns for the Spring Semester Looking to jumpstart your career in politics, public relations, or grassroots organizing? The Indiana Republican Party is now accepting applications for our Summer and Fall Internship Program! ✅ Gain hands-on experience in campaign strategy, communications, and event planning ✅ Work alongside state leaders and build valuable connections ✅ Make a real impact on Republican efforts in Indiana Apply Today! Discussion focused on implementation of President Trump’s recent Executive Order on preserving and protecting the integrity of American elections, and the Safeguard American Voter Eligibility (SAVE) Act currently moving through Congress. Treasurer Daniel Elliott "" President Trump to finish the U.S. Steel and Nippon Steel deal that was blocked under the Biden administration. Comptroller Elise Nieshalla 37 state financial officers to President Trump and Congress, calling for the restoration of fiscal solvency. Attorney General Todd Rokita "" 51 bipartisan Attorneys General of the Anti-Robocall Multistate Litigation Task Force in notifying nine voice service providers that they may be violating state and federal laws by continuing to route allegedly unlawful robocalls across their networks. Kramer. Judge Kramer passed away suddenly on Friday, March 28, 2025, at Ascension Resurrection Hospital in Chicago. Kramer was 70 and resided in Ligonier, Indiana. Congressman Jim Baird "" the Small Modular Reactor Commercialization Act of 2025 (SMRCA) to secure the United States’ preeminent position in commercializing and industrializing grid-scale small modular reactor (SMR) technology. This common-sense legislation ensures that only U.S. citizens can vote in federal elections. Congresswoman Erin Houchin Jefferson and Switzerland counties to meet with local officials and assess recent flooding, saying, "'I’m very grateful for their dedication and inspired by the resilience of these communities." | SEA 74: Provides that an individual who is: (1) reasonably believed to be suffering from a health condition which is the direct result of alcohol consumption; and (2) assisted by a person who requested emergency medical assistance for the individual; is immune from prosecution for certain crimes. SEA 95: Allows the state, a state agency, or a political subdivision (public employer) to be reimbursed for the costs of employing and training a law enforcement officer by a public employer that subsequently employs the officer. Provides that the reimbursement amount decreases over time and is eliminated three years after the officer is certified by the law enforcement training board to act as an officer. Allows a public employer to require, as a condition of hiring, that the individual enter into a contract or agreement to reimburse the public employer for employment and training costs, if the individual is subsequently hired by a private or governmental employer that is not defined by statute as a public employer. SEA 144: Requires in a county other than Marion County, that the county police department or a designee of the county police department make an annual presentation regarding the county police force pension trust and trust fund to the county council. SEA 218: Amends the information that must be provided by a supervising agency in quarterly reports to the local justice reinvestment advisory council. Requires the statewide justice reinvestment advisory council to compile the quarterly reports and electronically transmit an annual report to the legislative council and to the judicial conference of Indiana not later than May 1(instead of March 15) of each year. SEA 219: Provides that a person who, not having a contractual interest in the property, knowingly or intentionally enters the real property of another person after having been denied entry by the other person, that person's agent, or a law enforcement officer acting on behalf of the other person or their agent, commits criminal trespass, a Class A misdemeanor. Provides that a person who, not having a contractual interest in the property, knowingly or intentionally refuses to leave the real property of another person after having been asked to leave by the other person, that person's agent, or a law enforcement officer acting on behalf of the other person or the other person's agent, commits criminal trespass, a Class A misdemeanor. SEA 426: Expands IURC oversight to include certain water utilities with repeated environmental violations, updates utility acquisition and rate recovery rules, and protects utilities from damage claims if water meets regulatory standards. Also restricts the Department of Natural Resources from ordering dam closures that would affect public water service or private wells. SEA 457: Sets guidelines and requirements for carbon dioxide transmission pipeline certification, including contractor qualifications and fee reallocations. Updates regulations on carbon sequestration projects, establishes related fees and penalties, authorizes investigatory well permits, and redirects various revenues to the state general fund. SEA 486: Requires sheriffs to help eligible individuals apply for Medicaid before release from certain facilities and ensures continuity of Medicaid services for juveniles post-release. Also includes insurer response timelines, Medicaid claim rules, creation of a Medicaid beneficiary advisory commission, updated child care provider training requirements, and changes to advisory council memberships. HEA 1016: Provides for the appointment of a fire chief, or the fire chief's designee, to a county school safety commission. HEA 1033: Changes the maximum amount of retainage for certain state and local public works projects from: (1) 10% to 6% of the dollar value of all work satisfactorily completed until the public work is 50% complete; and (2) 5% to 3% of the dollar value of all work satisfactorily completed until the public work is substantially complete. Removes the requirement of a minimum amount of retainage for certain state and local public works projects. HEA 1065: Allows an organization or entity to establish and administer a cancer clinical trial participation program for the purpose of providing payments to a participant for certain costs incurred by the participant while participating in a cancer clinical trial. Sets forth program requirements and participant eligibility. HEA 1099: Provides that the maximum age for a safe haven infant is 60 days of age. Repeals the definition of "abandoned child". Amends the definition of "foster youth". Makes conforming changes. HEA 1102: Removes language restricting school corporations from entering into a contract with a religiously affiliated nonprofit preschool program. HEA 1137: Requires a court to expunge certain records related to the red flag law if the court finds that an individual is not dangerous, and permits a court to expunge certain records related to the red flag law if the court finds that an individual previously found dangerous is no longer dangerous. HEA 1148: Provides that a registration or certificate of a birth or stillbirth is open to public inspection and copying upon the request of any person that occurs 99 years (instead of 75 years) after the record is created. HEA 1200: Provides that if a prescriber issues a prescription for an opioid for administration through an intrathecal pump or epidural pain pump: (1) the prescription may not exceed a 180 day supply; and (2) the patient may not be required to have a face-to-face visit with the prescriber more than once every 180 days unless the prescriber determines an earlier follow up visit is medically necessary. HEA 1320: Provides that an individual who completes a United States Department of Labor registered apprenticeship program for barbers or manicurists is eligible to take the licensure examination of the applicable profession. HEA 1554: Specifies that the penalty for a operating a motor vehicle while under a lifetime forfeiture of driving privileges is: (1) a Level 6 felony, if the forfeiture occurred before July 1, 2015; and (2) a Level 5 felony, if the forfeiture occurred after June 30, 2015. HEA 1572: Adds speech-language pathology assistant to the definition of "practitioner" for purposes of the provision of telehealth. Specifies services included in the definition of "audiology". Allows for the sale, use, provision of customer service, or distribution of an over-the-counter hearing aid without the fitting of the hearing aid by a hearing aid dealer who has been issued a certificate of registration or a licensed audiologist. HEA 1626: Requires the following to be included in a custody order: (1) a trial court shall include the court's findings of fact and conclusions of law on which the custody order is based; and (2) an appellate decision shall include the facts upon which the appellate court relied to affirm or reverse an order. SEA 127: Prohibits a county, city, or town from adopting or enforcing an ordinance or resolution that prohibits or imposes requirements on certain battery charged security devices. SEA 221: Changes certain references from "auditor of state" to: (1) "state comptroller (referred to as "auditor of state" in the Constitution of the State of Indiana)"; or (2) "state comptroller (auditor of state)". Requires "state comptroller (auditor of state)" to be listed on the 2026 general election ballot SEA 450: Renames "delegate" to "commissioner" for Indiana's representation at an Article V convention and sets appointment requirements, including residency, lobbying restrictions, and criminal background. It also outlines the selection of commissioners, chair appointment, ethical restrictions, and mandates a joint resolution limiting their authority to amend certain constitutional provisions. SEA 484: Sets forth requirements for recreational vehicle dealer agreements for the sale of recreational vehicles in Indiana. SEA 524: Allows an Indiana resident of a contiguous county to be appointed the head of the department of law. | Upcoming Events Across the State April 15: April 17: April 22: April 22: April 24: April 25: April 25: April 26: April 26: April 28: April 30: May 2: May 6: May 7: May 9: May 14: May 17: May 23: June 6: June 13: July 25: August 23: August 23: August 23: | | | | PAID FOR BY THE INDIANA REPUBLICAN STATE COMMITTEE. | | NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE. | | | **MAGA-enabled Trump and DOGE are hurting Virginians.** [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqvhsqD6hnNNbCP7JMDskKO3DcwO3FPpifEsk6R5UXJAqeoZfkhx0rJYxvzeQyPAOXxPzaHpN7uxnA66Ao0W-7EqZyFw6iLW1nc6uNsMh9U_6E3r-8BahUSFbwOkWs5ux9uHYLhNTsN9kaIrf60Bueyl_tQ2CnczxIpmWNbaFSEzRg9IdDkPZ0TcsG1j-C0fQTI-g1btC9S6hW5LBkY102ITPerGpO5Dq2ffEBqe_h2uMiIEs2K5jsyep-2cof-X1o5-JPLsuc1YxOEiPH1oEYWCXbb6eakI82XgWqY8PYR-vyWtbI683Vu1zidjLMRRNLA3F4QeCggffise_2tJYdP8/4fl/rP2eJgYWRbOrkfE20KHglA/h0/h001.FamyQ6TdwF78AaSG-TnrWYgOBIV4B65Mu6hIOKhAsNs) **[Will you pitch in to help Democrat Elizabeth Guzman defend Virginia from the destructive policies of Trump and MAGA Musk?](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqvhsqD6hnNNbCP7JMDskKO3DcwO3FPpifEsk6R5UXJAqV4L1yibpnnxAqS2wxuS2391adw55EaVet93JJXLwbcl4Y2X2ny7zvNHM4TiWSkviGD0mMOw0bwi-xezY7v7Np7V6rHoi5NJg7QYh1hSbKMuFczdljc4b8Amx_S5gG8P635Jawoi7M9U-7foj4Tl6hrl9muzyAdrQglKltuDWiX21ZiDYeKHPdvrw2I8H1hdIzZjSeOlFRTOuVGkMivqorsufqjh9LBPbggYEU6d5Z16DERxcJd5OWPX38m8qNaWsu-ggQpi6ue3zRpAnJrCJPsMirjVV3dvLDAJysMO-4BC/4fl/rP2eJgYWRbOrkfE20KHglA/h1/h001.dNWsL8L8kNfrZSgGYm03YvD-gFX_x9tr3Ru2ebmkyDI)**
* * *
Virginians want experienced, effective leaders who will stand up and fight back against the chaos unleashed by Trump and Musk's toxic DOGE initiatives.
Elizabeth Guzman IS that proven leader who will challenge Trump and Musk’s policies that have taken a chainsaw to the federal workforce, the backbone of Virginia's economy and the lifeline for countless families.
She’s a proven leader who is more than just a candidate; she's a dedicated champion for Virginians, fiercely defending federal employees, labor unions, working families, reproductive freedom, and democracy. She is on a mission to flip a crucial seat in the Virginia State House, standing strong for the values that matter most to our communities.
_As we inch closer to the pivotal elections in November, Virginia finds itself at the heart of a critical battle to safeguard our rights, freedoms, and economic future from a mad king and his minions._

clean_body: | | | MAGA-enabled Trump and DOGE are hurting Virginians. Virginians want experienced, effective leaders who will stand up and fight back against the chaos unleashed by Trump and Musk's toxic DOGE initiatives. Elizabeth Guzman IS that proven leader who will challenge Trump and Musk’s policies that have taken a chainsaw to the federal workforce, the backbone of Virginia's economy and the lifeline for countless families. She’s a proven leader who is more than just a candidate; she's a dedicated champion for Virginians, fiercely defending federal employees, labor unions, working families, reproductive freedom, and democracy. She is on a mission to flip a crucial seat in the Virginia State House, standing strong for the values that matter most to our communities. As we inch closer to the pivotal elections in November, Virginia finds itself at the heart of a critical battle to safeguard our rights, freedoms, and economic future from a mad king and his minions. Elizabeth Guzman is laser-focused on flipping HD-22 and tackling the urgent issues affecting Virginia’s working families. NOW is the time to rally for meaningful change. I wanted to send a quick note because today is the final day of our 48-hour fundraising boost. We hope that 1,000 people will support this campaign by tomorrow morning, and we only need 150 more folks to reach that goal.
If you’re reading this, can you chip in just $5, $25, $50, or even $100 to help power my campaign for Attorney General? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2402-q2-jg-launch?refcode=250412-emailam]

name: Team ITT PAC
subject: Kentucky deserves better than what the GOP and Musk are offering.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by In This Together PAC
body:
This is wrong.
This is outrageous, Peter.

_Elon Musk’s DOGE is trying to cut $149 million in critical health funding for Kentucky_ — funding that was already committed by Congress and is essential for public health programs like vaccinations, addiction treatment, and other vital services.

**This isn’t just cruel, it’s unlawful.**

That's why Governor Andy Beshear is speaking up against this reckless move. He’s made it clear that Kentucky won’t accept these cuts without a fight, and he is committed to doing everything he can to restore the funding Kentuckians were promised.

But let’s be real — if it weren’t for leaders like Andy, the GOP would be getting away with this unchecked. They think they can pull the rug out from under Kentuckians, and no one will notice. clean_body: This is wrong. This is outrageous, Peter. Elon Musk’s DOGE is trying to cut $149 million in critical health funding for Kentucky — funding that was already committed by Congress and is essential for public health programs like vaccinations, addiction treatment, and other vital services. This isn’t just cruel, it’s unlawful. That's why Governor Andy Beshear is speaking up against this reckless move. He’s made it clear that Kentucky won’t accept these cuts without a fight, and he is committed to doing everything he can to restore the funding Kentuckians were promised. But let’s be real — if it weren’t for leaders like Andy, the GOP would be getting away with this unchecked. They think they can pull the rug out from under Kentuckians, and no one will notice. ### Trump is lying the U.S. into another war, this time against Tren de Aragua, and this time waging it here
| | [Jonathan Larsen](https://substack.com/@jonathanlarsen)
---
| Apr 12
---
| [](https://substack.com/@jonathanlarsen)

_This guest column originally[appeared](https://substack.com/redirect/88ce4fed-5231-4344-bfb8-455ee22ec133?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) at The Fucking News, the Substack of journalist Jonathan Larsen, a veteran of ABC, CNN, and MSNBC, who served as executive producer of Up with Chris Hayes. You can subscribe to TFN [here](https://substack.com/redirect/70e1cfdc-80d9-440a-8c27-de38c02bea7f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)._
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/0b1fe37c-129b-48c8-b346-0a390f6b0173?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Alleged members of Tren de Aragua in an El Salvador prison in an undated photo distributed by the El Salvadoran government. (Uncredited photo / El Salvadoran [president's office](https://substack.com/redirect/3ae1688d-22ca-4036-a712-02fdcfbe598e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).)
Much of the focus on Pres. Donald Trump's campaign to remove alleged Venezuelan gang members from the U.S. has been on the mistakes: The innocents affected and the risible, amateur methodology behind it. All of which is concerning and damning, of course.
But there's virtual silence about the threat that this is Iraq all over again. On American soil this time.
And while journalists are doing great work pushing back on Trump's dubious claims, there's little scrutiny of just how closely his case against Tren de Aragua mirrors the dynamics that got us into Iraq.
In the early 2000s, the Bush administration did just what al Qaeda wanted and used the fear from 9/11 to increase U.S. militarization here and abroad. Most Democrats were in lockstep.
Then the Bush administration spotlighted bogus intelligence of Iraqi complicity and/or future threats of (non-existent) WMDs, punished dissenting voices, and ignored real intelligence in order to sell a traumatized American people on waging war against Iraq.
Today, the Trump administration is doing virtually the same thing: Ignoring real intelligence, elevating bogus intelligence, ginning up a casus belli. Except this time the war has been declared here.
**Blue Amp 's a simple idea: Insiders turned fearless outsiders **_**telling you what others won 't**_**. We 're un-bought; We Need Your Support.**_**Please become a paid subscriber**_
[ Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.Xa4nMUg-AUp-fXsh7-zFUYydKn-XZXAQEdHo2oH3edw?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=161188853)
This week, the Supreme Court said that Trump can use the Alien Enemies Act to remove accused members of Tren de Aragua (TdA) if the detainees get a chance to dispute their membership. Trump first claimed that right in a March 15 proclamation.
It's an extraordinary power rarely claimed, and never in peacetime. Its current use expands the predicates for it beyond precedent and the original intent.
Trump's assertions in that [proclamation](https://substack.com/redirect/6912b912-28c5-43c1-814a-8902a1424c90?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) were based on, yes, faulty intelligence. But also, faulty intelligence that very well may have been ginned up and/or cherry-picked to stoke fear and the false sense of emergency used to justify expanded presidential/dictatorial powers.
In a [story](https://substack.com/redirect/a8279e1f-dcb3-49e9-88c4-301fa7964db3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) posted March 20 and updated March 22, Charlie Savage and Julian E. Barnes of the New York Times revealed that most U.S. intelligence agencies disagreed with the claims Trump made, and had "moderate" confidence that they were false.
Specifically, Trump claimed that Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro is running criminal gangs which he is sending to the U.S. to wage war on us:
> > "Maduro leads the regime-sponsored enterprise Cartel de los Soles, which coordinates with and relies on TdA...
>> "TdA is undertaking hostile actions and conducting irregular warfare against the territory of the United States both directly and at the direction, clandestine or otherwise, of the Maduro regime..."
As Savage and Barnes revealed, the U.S. intelligence community -- _prior_ to Trump's proclamation -- had produced and circulated a document reporting "moderate" confidence that Tren de Aragua was neither run by the Venezuelan government nor committing crimes at the regime's behest.
Trump ignored the document's findings.
And the FBI was the only agency to dissent from the document, partially, arguing that TdA has connections to the Maduro government. That FBI finding[1](https://substack.com/redirect/8db0ff41-87d8-4e4b-832f-6f43760fe3c0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) was based on information the other agencies found not credible.
As one official told the Times, the existence of individual corrupt officials with ties to TdA doesn't prove a formal connection. (To give an analogy, it's like claiming the U.S. government was mob-run because some FBI agents took Mafia payoffs[2](https://substack.com/redirect/9e98bdfb-b839-4e78-a1c5-2f50e35335f3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).)
In fact, just as Iraq was found to be at odds with al Qaeda, Trump's own intelligence community reported that Tren de Aragua has had _gunfights_ with Venezuelan government security forces.
Trump's intelligence officials concluded that Tren de Aragua lack the resources and organization to carry out Venezuelan government orders. A Maduro minister applauded when the State Department in February designated Tren de Aragua a terrorist organization[3](https://substack.com/redirect/d6e2c5ab-138d-4ab9-abd5-92eb574e5db5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
The most specific evidence Trump could muster was a Maduro government official from the Aragua area -- and, as the Times noted, Trump omitted the fact that the official is no longer in Maduro's government and is now facing corruption charges.
The intelligence community that produced this assessment was now being run by Trump appointees.
The best the White House could do for a response was to tell the Times, "Multiple intelligence assessments are prepared on issues for a variety of reasons." Which is, of course, entirely non-responsive to the substance and point of the Times's revelations.
But the credibility of the link to Maduro is vital to Trump's claim to power. As the Times notes, the Alien Enemies Act bestows its extraordinary deportation powers -- or renditions, when detainees are sent elsewhere, such as El Salvador -- only in cases where a foreign government is involved.
No Maduro link means no Alien Enemies Act which means no extraordinary renditions.
So where did Trump get the intel to make the Maduro link?
The day after the Times story was published, the Miami Herald ran a wholly unskeptical [story](https://substack.com/redirect/fb9ef4b2-8312-4bb8-b602-4f44baaa4de4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) about the origins of Trump's intel. As far as I can tell, no one's yet connected the dots between the Herald's revelations of Trump's intel sources and what the Times exposed about Trump's shoddy, Iraq-quality intel.
Although I dinged the Herald for being unskeptical, there's no evidence they got any of their details wrong -- they just reported solely on one aspect of the intel picture. Not every article can include the full scope of the subject matter.
And the Herald story -- which was largely if not entirely ignored by corporate media -- revealed some extremely important information.
According to the Herald, prior to Trump's Jan. 20, 2025, inauguration, unnamed members of his transition got a presentation from an investigative team of former Venezuelan and U.S. officials about Tren de Aragua. This investigative team has continued to meet with "high-ranking" Trump officials, the Herald reported.
This team has also provided the Trump administration with supposed evidence of Maduro ties to Tren de Aragua, along with hundreds of names and photographs, which federal officials have been using in their hunt.
The team claims that the Maduro regime plans to send a total of 5,000 gang members here. Those already here include 300 who received paramilitary training in Venezuela, the team says.
"They put these 300 guys through that course and ... were deploying them into the United States to 20 locations, to 20 separate states," Gary Berntsen, the only team member identified by name, told the Herald.
But NPR [reports](https://substack.com/redirect/84c6bf9b-f2f2-44e8-8d0f-4fa0adf57a8e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that Tren de Aragua activity has occurred mostly in just five states: Colorado, Florida, Illinois, New York, and Texas. Aurora, CO, the city supposedly run by Tren de Aragua, has about a dozen members there, [say](https://substack.com/redirect/84c6bf9b-f2f2-44e8-8d0f-4fa0adf57a8e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the local police -- who presumably aren't (yet) working for Tren de Aragua.
Even beyond official pushback to the investigative team's findings, there are questions about the team itself.
For one thing, the former Venezuelan government officials on the team sound a lot like the Iraqi intel sources; former office-holders now on the outs who hope to regain power with the ouster of the new regime. Prior to the Iraq war, those sources had every motive for getting the U.S. to oust Saddam Hussein.
And the former American officials on this investigative team? The Herald describes them as having "deep connections to police and intelligence" in Venezuela. Without more info, we can't know whether these sources -- or _their_ sources -- might be motivated by ambition or rivalries within the Maduro regime.
In fairness, _any_ source worth having is likely to have a potential conflict of interest, but the point here is that we don't know. And, again, even the Trump intelligence community rejects their findings.
But let's look at the team member whose identity we do know. That'd be Gary Berntsen.
The Herald describes him as "a decorated former CIA station chief who headed the agency's unit searching for Osama Bin Laden in Afghanistan."
Which is true, but doesn't tell us much about what Berntsen's done in the two decades since Tora Bora. His company's [website](https://substack.com/redirect/539c6f6d-d62e-4b55-b2f4-00e72d5a2706?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), active at least through early 2023, says,
> > The Berntsen Group offers, on a global basis, timely, accurate and reliable investigative and security services. Our U.S. domestic staff consist of highly trained former senior U.S. intelligence and federal law enforcement personnel. Former foreign law enforcement, military officers and justice ministry personnel augment our services in offshore operations.
There's more. In 2013, Berntsen ran for Senate in New York, failing to win the Republican nomination to challenge Sen. Chuck Schumer (D-NY).
Berntsen was a Tea Party [candidate](https://substack.com/redirect/09eb8a84-9419-43b6-bb96-0270b3b7f6d1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). He [campaigned](https://substack.com/redirect/09eb8a84-9419-43b6-bb96-0270b3b7f6d1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on the threat of illegal immigration.
Nevertheless, it was the deficit, Berntsen said in 2013, that constituted "the gravest danger we have been in since World War II."
None of which tells us whether Berntsen's intel now is credible. But it doesn't have to be to help the cause by providing enough fodder to defend Trump's stance or at least muddy the waters, giving cover for proclamations and judicial rulings.
Sure enough, last month a right-wing site published an in-your-face-NYT [piece](https://substack.com/redirect/ae24ff85-b8ca-497b-a01d-cbd24211a201?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) citing the Herald's reporting as proof of Maduro ties to TdA. The site is literally [sponsored](https://substack.com/redirect/0783c1c9-89a6-42e5-8696-011ff883a3f0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by the John Birch Society.

1 Trump loyalist Kash Patel was sworn in as FBI director on Feb. 21, 2025.
[2](https://substack.com/redirect/23890778-fd41-475d-b3d7-90080e96fa1d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) TFN acknowledges pre-emptively that some Newsfuckers may object that, "The U.S. government _is_ mob-run! Wake up, sheeple!" We hear you. We're just making an analogy!
[3](https://substack.com/redirect/d5b339d8-c024-45f3-92a5-404ef78f67c4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) The Times notes that this was unprecedented, as previous designations only targeted groups motivated by specific ideologies.

(Uncredited photo / El Salvadoran .) Much of the focus on Pres. Donald Trump's campaign to remove alleged Venezuelan gang members from the U.S. has been on the mistakes: The innocents affected and the risible, amateur methodology behind it. All of which is concerning and damning, of course. But there's virtual silence about the threat that this is Iraq all over again. On American soil this time. And while journalists are doing great work pushing back on Trump's dubious claims, there's little scrutiny of just how closely his case against Tren de Aragua mirrors the dynamics that got us into Iraq. In the early 2000s, the Bush administration did just what al Qaeda wanted and used the fear from 9/11 to increase U.S. militarization here and abroad. Most Democrats were in lockstep. Then the Bush administration spotlighted bogus intelligence of Iraqi complicity and/or future threats of (non-existent) WMDs, punished dissenting voices, and ignored real intelligence in order to sell a traumatized American people on waging war against Iraq. Today, the Trump administration is doing virtually the same thing: Ignoring real intelligence, elevating bogus intelligence, ginning up a casus belli. Except this time the war has been declared here. Blue Amp 's a simple idea: Insiders turned fearless outsiders telling you what others won 't. We 're un-bought; We Need Your Support.Please become a paid subscriber This week, the Supreme Court said that Trump can use the Alien Enemies Act to remove accused members of Tren de Aragua (TdA) if the detainees get a chance to dispute their membership. Trump first claimed that right in a March 15 proclamation. It's an extraordinary power rarely claimed, and never in peacetime. Its current use expands the predicates for it beyond precedent and the original intent. Trump's assertions in that were based on, yes, faulty intelligence. But also, faulty intelligence that very well may have been ginned up and/or cherry-picked to stoke fear and the false sense of emergency used to justify expanded presidential/dictatorial powers. In a posted March 20 and updated March 22, Charlie Savage and Julian E. Barnes of the New York Times revealed that most U.S. intelligence agencies disagreed with the claims Trump made, and had "moderate" confidence that they were false. Specifically, Trump claimed that Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro is running criminal gangs which he is sending to the U.S. to wage war on us: > "Maduro leads the regime-sponsored enterprise Cartel de los Soles, which coordinates with and relies on TdA... > "TdA is undertaking hostile actions and conducting irregular warfare against the territory of the United States both directly and at the direction, clandestine or otherwise, of the Maduro regime..." As Savage and Barnes revealed, the U.S. intelligence community -- prior to Trump's proclamation -- had produced and circulated a document reporting "moderate" confidence that Tren de Aragua was neither run by the Venezuelan government nor committing crimes at the regime's behest. Trump ignored the document's findings. And the FBI was the only agency to dissent from the document, partially, arguing that TdA has connections to the Maduro government. That FBI finding was based on information the other agencies found not credible. As one official told the Times, the existence of individual corrupt officials with ties to TdA doesn't prove a formal connection. (To give an analogy, it's like claiming the U.S. government was mob-run because some FBI agents took Mafia payoffs.) In fact, just as Iraq was found to be at odds with al Qaeda, Trump's own intelligence community reported that Tren de Aragua has had gunfights with Venezuelan government security forces. Trump's intelligence officials concluded that Tren de Aragua lack the resources and organization to carry out Venezuelan government orders. A Maduro minister applauded when the State Department in February designated Tren de Aragua a terrorist organization. The most specific evidence Trump could muster was a Maduro government official from the Aragua area -- and, as the Times noted, Trump omitted the fact that the official is no longer in Maduro's government and is now facing corruption charges. The intelligence community that produced this assessment was now being run by Trump appointees. The best the White House could do for a response was to tell the Times, "Multiple intelligence assessments are prepared on issues for a variety of reasons." Which is, of course, entirely non-responsive to the substance and point of the Times's revelations. But the credibility of the link to Maduro is vital to Trump's claim to power. As the Times notes, the Alien Enemies Act bestows its extraordinary deportation powers -- or renditions, when detainees are sent elsewhere, such as El Salvador -- only in cases where a foreign government is involved. No Maduro link means no Alien Enemies Act which means no extraordinary renditions. So where did Trump get the intel to make the Maduro link? The day after the Times story was published, the Miami Herald ran a wholly unskeptical about the origins of Trump's intel. As far as I can tell, no one's yet connected the dots between the Herald's revelations of Trump's intel sources and what the Times exposed about Trump's shoddy, Iraq-quality intel. Although I dinged the Herald for being unskeptical, there's no evidence they got any of their details wrong -- they just reported solely on one aspect of the intel picture. Not every article can include the full scope of the subject matter. And the Herald story -- which was largely if not entirely ignored by corporate media -- revealed some extremely important information. According to the Herald, prior to Trump's Jan. 20, 2025, inauguration, unnamed members of his transition got a presentation from an investigative team of former Venezuelan and U.S. officials about Tren de Aragua. This investigative team has continued to meet with "high-ranking" Trump officials, the Herald reported. This team has also provided the Trump administration with supposed evidence of Maduro ties to Tren de Aragua, along with hundreds of names and photographs, which federal officials have been using in their hunt. The team claims that the Maduro regime plans to send a total of 5,000 gang members here. Those already here include 300 who received paramilitary training in Venezuela, the team says. "They put these 300 guys through that course and ... were deploying them into the United States to 20 locations, to 20 separate states," Gary Berntsen, the only team member identified by name, told the Herald. But NPR that Tren de Aragua activity has occurred mostly in just five states: Colorado, Florida, Illinois, New York, and Texas. Aurora, CO, the city supposedly run by Tren de Aragua, has about a dozen members there, the local police -- who presumably aren't (yet) working for Tren de Aragua. Even beyond official pushback to the investigative team's findings, there are questions about the team itself. For one thing, the former Venezuelan government officials on the team sound a lot like the Iraqi intel sources; former office-holders now on the outs who hope to regain power with the ouster of the new regime. Prior to the Iraq war, those sources had every motive for getting the U.S. to oust Saddam Hussein. And the former American officials on this investigative team? The Herald describes them as having "deep connections to police and intelligence" in Venezuela. Without more info, we can't know whether these sources -- or their sources -- might be motivated by ambition or rivalries within the Maduro regime. In fairness, any source worth having is likely to have a potential conflict of interest, but the point here is that we don't know. And, again, even the Trump intelligence community rejects their findings. But let's look at the team member whose identity we do know. That'd be Gary Berntsen. The Herald describes him as "a decorated former CIA station chief who headed the agency's unit searching for Osama Bin Laden in Afghanistan." Which is true, but doesn't tell us much about what Berntsen's done in the two decades since Tora Bora. His company's , active at least through early 2023, says, > The Berntsen Group offers, on a global basis, timely, accurate and reliable investigative and security services. Our U.S. domestic staff consist of highly trained former senior U.S. intelligence and federal law enforcement personnel. Former foreign law enforcement, military officers and justice ministry personnel augment our services in offshore operations. There's more. In 2013, Berntsen ran for Senate in New York, failing to win the Republican nomination to challenge Sen. Chuck Schumer (D-NY). Berntsen was a Tea Party . He on the threat of illegal immigration. Nevertheless, it was the deficit, Berntsen said in 2013, that constituted "the gravest danger we have been in since World War II." None of which tells us whether Berntsen's intel now is credible. But it doesn't have to be to help the cause by providing enough fodder to defend Trump's stance or at least muddy the waters, giving cover for proclamations and judicial rulings. Sure enough, last month a right-wing site published an in-your-face-NYT citing the Herald's reporting as proof of Maduro ties to TdA. The site is literally by the John Birch Society. Blue Amp 's a simple idea: Insiders turned fearless outsiders telling you what others won 't. We 're un-bought; We Need Your Support.Please become a paid subscriber * 1 Trump loyalist Kash Patel was sworn in as FBI director on Feb. 21, 2025. TFN acknowledges pre-emptively that some Newsfuckers may object that, "The U.S. government is mob-run! Wake up, sheeple!" We hear you. We're just making an analogy! The Times notes that this was unprecedented, as previous designations only targeted groups motivated by specific ideologies. You're currently a free subscriber to . Hi Friend,
We’re not going to fix the damage Trump is doing to our nation, economy, and the rule of law unless we change the institution that continues to green-light it.

This is Alan Cohn from Term Limit the Court, the national campaign working with 75 members of the US House and Senate to end lifetime appointments to the Supreme Court and enact 18-year term limits.
Have you noticed even when SCOTUS appears to rule against Trump, they leave him enough wiggle room to do it anyway?

It’s not a coincidence, but it is why we need to retire justices like Clarence Thomas and Samuel Alito, who ignore the rule of law and the Constitution in favor of their radical personal politics.

We have the opportunity. It’s called the TERM Act. Please help us flip the House and Senate by backing candidates for the midterms and pledging to support it.

SCOTUS has robbed women of making their own healthcare decisions. They granted immunity from prosecution to Trump. They’re allowing him to fire federal employees and deport immigrants without due process.

The Republicans in Congress won’t stop him. SCOTUS won’t stop him.

We’ve created a movement to reform how we appoint justices to the Supreme Court and how long they can stay there.
**[>>>What we need is you!](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105722196/539176395/-859072474?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS8xMDE4NzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMDQzMjdmMzUtYzgxNy1mMDExLThiM2QtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZmI3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=c0K30NKAuADIp4ADfQMqgCiFCt5wciEGAKv68TUyAGY=)**
Alan Cohn
Term Limit the Court

The American people have lost confidence in the Supreme Court after a series of rulings that strike at the core of our freedoms. _There are term limits for a President. Term limits for many governors and state legislators. And the time has come for term limits for members of the Supreme Court._

**Now, we're counting on your help to provide the resources we need to work directly with members of Congress and their staff to support the introduction of term-limit legislation and raise awareness about this legislative solution to the growing distrust in the Supreme Court.**

Dear Derek,
It’s been a month since I launched my campaign for California Governor, and I’m so grateful for all the support I’ve received.
**Thanks to folks like you, we’ve built real momentum in this race against 10+ declared candidates. We have a strong foundation, but we can’t let up.[Will you chip in $5 today?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_kp_20250412_1_ak?refcode=em20250412-39634&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=39634_3422442_XoM-C3&akid=39634%2E3422442%2EXoM-C3)**

I know we can win this race, as long as I have Porter Supporters like you with me.
I’m going to be working hard through next year’s election to listen to Californians and to share my vision for how we tackle families’ biggest challenges, especially the cost of living.
I’d be honored to have you on my team, Derek.

Katie

name: Scalise, Steve
subject: I am NOT “canceling” your GOP Membership
disclaimer_text: Paid for by TEAM SCALISE, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and to benefit SCALISE FOR CONGRESS and EYE OF THE TIGER PAC
body:
You are not an ATM, you are a member of the team.
| |
---
| | | Let me start with this:

I have noticed how dedicated you are to our MAGA movement - and I thank you for it.

This is Steve Scalise. I wanted to make sure you know that I've received your feedback, appreciated you signing our petitions, and made sure any financial contributions were spent wisely AND in races where they make the biggest impact.

_We would be NOWHERE without your support and guidance. _

Democrats would probably have total control if it weren't for folks like you doing what they can to help.

I can promise you this - I will never forget it.

That's why I'll never threaten to "remove" you from the Republican Party or "revoke" your GOP membership if you don't donate. That stuff drives me crazy. **You are not an ATM, you are a valued and respected member of the team.**

But that doesn't mean I won't email or text you from time to time, but I promise to message you only when it's absolutely necessary.

That's why I emailed you when we were shaping our first 100 days... I emailed you about the Special Elections... And I emailed you about the DNC suing President Trump...

_**[Your input and support helped us move quickly, win the Special Elections, and advance our MAGA agenda. Thank you again for stepping up when we needed you most.](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=458878145e5d4dcba39acba184870cc1&_e=-RFsk-yo-mPOXG3Ph6cThTvOoRJMZcvX6OlsUrsX0-cmq8y9Vag5OHXonhTDlVEblEX1pCd-A0GeCkGqRdjacpRpAPE1-KIXB_3pNyq0pWgP60K3pSUYbP3vZv1vovZXk5Ml2RBHJs86kS0rqhu1eaULKbZ-5-tEXqZG8IeMGrO5sUDaOYZbe82w5v8etNAFxKcvvC2lR5xdicmvkRxiCMdU2-LqgkWsp_iat3b7dtqlFe9Ykj24iRYP34baB00vIZO556jBdoEczZVlO1vigy6iTnvHwdYRuDwB2rKfSeBJ-GuWU98bGA1LPPWRe-15k3TjuuoMYKBi_T7V1fMD5pxkSjUuTOaEdyycYDujitRdyNJcs8SRHPzlw5IZpW6y26InLkZSVV3YZCCzJY9Gog%3D%3D "Your input and support helped us move quickly, win the Special Elections, and advance our MAGA agenda. Thank you again for stepping up when we needed you most. ")**_

Unfortunately, it's not all good news.

Our candidate in FL-06 was outraised 10-to-1, we lost the Wisconsin Supreme Court race, and the Democrat Machine is emboldened like I've NEVER SEEN BEFORE! Liberal money poured in seeing these elections as a chance to sabotage President Trump's agenda.

_**[So if- and only if - you can afford to do so, would you be willing to pitch in a few bucks to help us rebuild our warchest?](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=458878145e5d4dcba39acba184870cc1&_e=-RFsk-yo-mPOXG3Ph6cThTvOoRJMZcvX6OlsUrsX0-cmq8y9Vag5OHXonhTDlVEblEX1pCd-A0GeCkGqRdjacpRpAPE1-KIXB_3pNyq0pWgP60K3pSUYbP3vZv1vovZXk5Ml2RBHJs86kS0rqhu1eaULKbZ-5-tEXqZG8IeMGrO5sUDaOYZbe82w5v8etNAFxKcvvC2lR5xdicmvkRxiCMdU2-LqgkWsp_iat3b7dtqlFe9Ykj24iRYP34baB00vfNWyPhMnowo23aYatZ450GE9w93lf7Z51cIcfdmpn-bwFKS_9ce-6zgHkI6wV7QHVGnRlzkF8iK7XE4ddNbMHDjMdT9p3EsVNBOfDobKnz66cbpjoJmkvjCZPhC3P7yVpOaaySFipGHHSKDW1Zo9Yw%3D%3D "So if – and only if – you can afford to do so, would you be willing to pitch in a few bucks to help us rebuild our warchest? ")**_

Here's some intel that made my heart sink...
* A House Democrat Super PAC is unloading **$50M** into toss-up districts
* The Liberal outraised our candidate in the Wisconsin race by **2****00%**
* Now **two** GOP-held House seats are in serious jeopardy

The Democrat Machine is channeling all the over-the-top Liberal outrage toward Elon Musk, President Trump's deportations, and our quick work in the House into raising campaign funds to elect Democrats.

In other words, we urgently need some more support right now. _**[It would mean a great deal to me personally if you'd be willing to help.](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=458878145e5d4dcba39acba184870cc1&_e=-RFsk-yo-mPOXG3Ph6cThTvOoRJMZcvX6OlsUrsX0-cmq8y9Vag5OHXonhTDlVEblEX1pCd-A0GeCkGqRdjacpRpAPE1-KIXB_3pNyq0pWgP60K3pSUYbP3vZv1vovZXk5Ml2RBHJs86kS0rqhu1eaULKbZ-5-tEXqZG8IeMGrO5sUDaOYZbe82w5v8etNAFxKcvvC2lR5xdicmvkRxiCMdU2-LqgkWsp_iat3b7dtqlFe9Ykj24iRYP34baB00v0uoKHdnvBfH6oXUIhuARwXTAMJuDPj1UWJRYxDIrZk7aFLDnM-i4EB2RjroKALPfTHYPjNB6LoxXbCE_WOkPdGeD7TJq_kYY7p9dSER_ob6TUhPQoIiagD1YSYpyKOGPFJseapQzRYkZ8pNZpE0GEg%3D%3D "It would mean a great deal to me personally if you’d be willing to help.")**_

clean_body: You are not an ATM, you are a member of the team. | | | | | Let me start with this: I have noticed how dedicated you are to our MAGA movement - and I thank you for it. This is Steve Scalise. I wanted to make sure you know that I've received your feedback, appreciated you signing our petitions, and made sure any financial contributions were spent wisely AND in races where they make the biggest impact. We would be NOWHERE without your support and guidance. Democrats would probably have total control if it weren't for folks like you doing what they can to help. I can promise you this - I will never forget it. That's why I'll never threaten to "remove" you from the Republican Party or "revoke" your GOP membership if you don't donate. That stuff drives me crazy. You are not an ATM, you are a valued and respected member of the team. But that doesn't mean I won't email or text you from time to time, but I promise to message you only when it's absolutely necessary. That's why I emailed you when we were shaping our first 100 days... I emailed you about the Special Elections... And I emailed you about the DNC suing President Trump... "Your input and support helped us move quickly, win the Special Elections, and advance our MAGA agenda. Thank you again for stepping up when we needed you most. " Unfortunately, it's not all good news. Our candidate in FL-06 was outraised 10-to-1, we lost the Wisconsin Supreme Court race, and the Democrat Machine is emboldened like I've NEVER SEEN BEFORE! Liberal money poured in seeing these elections as a chance to sabotage President Trump's agenda. "So if – and only if – you can afford to do so, would you be willing to pitch in a few bucks to help us rebuild our warchest? " Here's some intel that made my heart sink... A House Democrat Super PAC is unloading $50M into toss-up districts The Liberal outraised our candidate in the Wisconsin race by 200% Now two GOP-held House seats are in serious jeopardy The Democrat Machine is channeling all the over-the-top Liberal outrage toward Elon Musk, President Trump's deportations, and our quick work in the House into raising campaign funds to elect Democrats. In other words, we urgently need some more support right now. "It would mean a great deal to me personally if you’d be willing to help." | | | "PITCH IN HERE" Either way, thanks for all you do. Peter,

The road to re-election just got a whole lot more challenging: **The experts at _Sabato’s Crystal Ball_ recently ranked CA-27 as one of the most competitive districts in the nation. We’re dangerously close to becoming a toss-up race.**

It has always been a challenge to re-elect a Democrat in this critical district – and with so much at stake for our communities and our democracy, the time to make it happen is now.

If these last few months have shown us anything, it’s that there is no limit to what Republicans will do to tear down our democracy. Trump and his friends in Congress are killing our future and using corruption to uplift billionaires and corporations instead of working families.

Meanwhile, I’m fighting for the future and working against corruption in Congress. But there’s no way around it: **The best way to hold this administration accountable is to win control of the House.**

name: Melanie Stansbury
subject: Here’s how I’m holding Elon Musk accountable
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Blue Amp Action PAC
body:
I introduced the “Nobody Elected Elon Musk Act” to hold Elon accountable for his attacks on our federal government.
| | | |
---
| | | | |
---
| _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T373f6c3a-c3c8-477d-bfb1-01bd4bf536cb/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
---
[](https://default.salsalabs.org/T7050dbeb-b322-498e-96e7-7f83e6a9e063/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
| Peter, Melanie Stansbury here. We’re currently seeing an unprecedented attack on our federal agencies and our federal workers from Donald Trump and unelected billionaire Elon Musk. [In a moment, I’m going to ask you to split a contribution between my campaign and Blue Amp Action »](https://default.salsalabs.org/T8f996a17-4679-40e4-836b-4065d78cc471/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) But first, I want to outline for you the threats we’re facing and what I am doing to fight back. Donald Trump and Elon Musk have gone after funding for education and cancer research, taken a sledgehammer to the EPA, and eliminated domestic watchdogs like the CFPB that stop banks from ripping off consumers. It’s hard to imagine Medicare, Social Security, and Medicaid won’t be next on their list. Americans are scared – and rightfully so. That’s why I introduced the “Nobody Elected Elon Musk Act” – to hold Elon accountable for his attacks on our federal government. Standing by is not an option. We have to disrupt the process as much as we can, fight back, and hold Republicans accountable. Blue Amp Action and I are leading the charge – but we need you on our team. [Can I count on you to rush a gift, split between my campaign and Blue Amp Action today to support our fight to hold Elon Musk accountable? We refuse to let an oligarch gut our government from the inside out »](https://default.salsalabs.org/T50c0c98a-a802-4c28-8ce2-e27366a6c6f6/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T15babed7-53cf-4c5d-9126-a856c2104e27/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
